findings.evidence: `rg -n "theorem characteristicWeight_conjugate_neg|theorem characteristicWeightNumerator_neg_neg"` shows these at lines 91 and 113 with no attribute, while nearby normal forms such as `characteristicWeight_zero`, `characteristicWeight_single`, and `characteristicWeight_canonical_single` are `@[simp]` in `Weight.lean`.
findings.fix: Add `@[simp]` to `characteristicWeight_conjugate_neg`; also tag `characteristicWeightNumerator_neg_neg` if the numerator API is intended as a public normal form.
findings.issue: The main normal-form rewrite `characteristicWeight_conjugate_neg` is untagged, so `simp` will not reduce `P.characteristicWeight (P.conjugate k) (-x)` to `P.characteristicWeight k x`.

findings.evidence: `Conjugation.lean:136` declares the conjugate canonical single lemma without attributes; `Weight.lean:180` and `Weight.lean:186` tag the corresponding basis-weight normal forms with `@[simp]`.
findings.issue: The concrete canonical basis value `characteristicWeight_conjugate_canonical_single` is parallel to the existing canonical single-value API but lacks the matching `@[simp]` annotation.

summary: The exposed conjugation surface has the right characteristic lemmas, but its normal-form API is not registered with automation. Downstream users will have to name the new lemmas manually for the central conjugation rewrites and the canonical basis value.
